---
title: "Download"
toc: false
---

## Windows installer

GenoSuite ships as a **self-contained Windows installer** — it bundles
everything it needs, so no separate R installation is required.

[Download GenoSuite-Setup.exe](https://github.com/mqfarooqi1/GenoSuite/releases/latest){.btn-genosuite}

The latest installer is published on the
**[GitHub Releases](https://github.com/mqfarooqi1/GenoSuite/releases)** page.

::: callout-note
The installer is large (~200–300 MB) because it includes a private copy of R
and all required packages. This is what makes it self-contained: install once,
run offline.
:::

### Installing

1. Download `GenoSuite-Setup.exe`.
2. Double-click it and follow the prompts (you may see a Windows SmartScreen
   notice for a new publisher — choose **More info → Run anyway**).
3. Launch **GenoSuite** from the Start menu.

### System requirements

- Windows 10 or 11 (64-bit)
- A modern web browser (Edge, Chrome, or Firefox) for the interface
- ~700 MB free disk space after installation

## Run from source

Developers and macOS/Linux users can run GenoSuite from source with R:

```r
install.packages(c("shiny", "bslib", "DT", "ggplot2", "ape", "vegan",
                   "glmnet", "ranger", "xgboost"))
install.packages("GSbench", repos = "https://mqfarooqi1.r-universe.dev")

# from the cloned repository
shiny::runApp("app")
```

The full source is on
**[GitHub](https://github.com/mqfarooqi1/GenoSuite)** under the MIT License.
